documentation.readme: Chisel is a fast TCP/UDP tunnel, transported over HTTP, secured via SSH. Single executable including both client and server. Written in Go (golang). Chisel is mainly useful for passing through firewalls, though it can also be used to provide a secure endpoint into your network.
usage_sections: Encrypted connections using the SSH protocol (via crypto/ssh)
Authenticated connections; authenticated client connections with a users config file, authenticated server connections with fingerprint matching.
Clients can create multiple tunnel endpoints over one TCP connection
Clients can optionally pass through SOCKS or HTTP CONNECT proxies
Reverse port forwarding (Connections go through the server and out the client)

arguments.name: -established
arguments.description: Clients can create multiple tunnel endpoints over one TCP connection

arguments.name: -exits
arguments.description: UDP exit nodes no longer break or leak past 100 concurrent flows (`CHISEL_UDP_MAX_CONNS`)

arguments.name: -stage
arguments.description: tagging builds a draft GitHub release plus version-tagged images, and publishing the draft promotes the Docker `latest` / `X` / `X.Y` tags

arguments.name: -to-end
arguments.description: the CDN cannot read or modify tunneled traffic. Keep `--keepalive` at its `25s` default to stay under CDN idle timeouts, and note that proxies which strip `Upgrade` headers cannot

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: docker run --rm -it jpillora/chisel --help; go install github.com/jpillora/chisel@latest.
